arts.ttl: Berta may hold key to Partey's Arsenal future
arts.sum: Arsenal midfielder Thomas Partey's future remains unclear but recently appointed sporting director Andrea Berta may have the key to unlocking the uncertainty surrounding the player's contractual impasse.

arts.ttl: UEFA could make THREE changes to Champions League format after Arsenal complaint
arts.sum: The Champions League's new 36-team format has been in place for the first time this season but some changes have been floated before the end of the current campaign

arts.ttl: Arsenal v Crystal Palace: Premier League – live
arts.sum: Premier League updates from the 8pm BST kick-off Live scores | Tables | And read today’s Football Daily Both managers have picked surprisingly strong teams. Arsenal do rest Bukayo Saka, who had an injury scare at Ipswich, and Mikel Merino isn’t in the squad. No word yet whether he’s been given the night off or whether they is an injury. Apart from that it’s the strongest available team. Myles Lewis-Skelly, Jurrien Timber, Thomas Partey and Raheem Sterling return in place of Oleksandr Zinchenko, Ben White, Merino and Saka. We cannot think in those terms. I think when the players are fit and they are available and they want to play, they have to play. They are at their best when they are playing and they have consistency in their performances. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Team News: Glasner names starting XI for Arsenal test
arts.sum: Oliver Glasner has made four changes to his Crystal Palace starting XI to face Arsenal at the Emirates Stadium on Wednesday evening.

arts.ttl: Arsenal and Chelsea handed Xavi Simons transfer update as new RB Leipzig 'stance' emerges
arts.sum: The report claims that Simons is valued at around €80million (£68.5million) going into the summer transfer window, but the Bundesliga club are yet to receive any official offers for the playmaker. RB Leipzig have reportedly named their asking price for playmaker Xavi Simons this summer, amid reported interest from Arsenal and Chelsea .

arts.ttl: How Arsenal aim to learn US lessons for Emirates to aid Mikel Arteta and Andrea Berta plans
arts.sum: “While in North America it is obviously important to grow your brands and to grow your teams, certainly in the Premier League, the more revenue you can drive does affect the product that you can put on the pitch,” Demoff said. The company also has extensive real estate attached to their sporting assets such as the Gunners’ Emirates Stadium home, the So-Fi Stadium where the LA Rams play, and the Nuggets’ Ball Arena home.

arts.ttl: Arsenal v Crystal Palace LIVE: Team news and updates as Gunners look to delay Liverpool title celebrations
arts.sum: Arsenal face Palace with little left to play for domestically, though they will be hoping to avoid injuries ahead of the Champions League semi-finals
